avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche danger in all regions is within the upper range of danger level 4 (high). A critical avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area. In particular along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Very dangerous avalanche situation with three overlapping avalanche problems: fresh snow, wind-blown snow and persistent weak layers.
Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Avalanches can in many places be released very easily. As a consequence of new snow and strong wind natural avalanches are to be expected, even large ones. The avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach very large size. In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can reach as far as the valley bottom and endanger transportation routes that are exposed. The emptied starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of snowfall and wind.

The conditions are very critical for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities outside marked and open pistes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Wednesday: 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the late morning above approximately 1600 m.
40 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Thursday above approximately 2000 m.
The weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall are bonding poorly with the old snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The large quantity of fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects represent the main danger. With the end of the intense precipitation, the natural avalanche activity will decrease.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs: From starting zones at higher altitudes large natural avalanches must be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and stormy weather the wind slabs will increase in size substantially as the day progresses. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as at a distance from ridgelines above the tree line.

New snow and wind slabs can in many places be released easily and reach large size.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and larger. These places are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

Additionally avalanches can also release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach very large size.
Exposed parts of transportation routes can be endangered in some localities in this instance.

snowpackStructure.comment:
15 to 25 cm of snow fell on Wednesday above approximately 1800 m. Over a wide area 25 to 50 cm of snow will fall on Thursday above approximately 2000 m. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northwesterly directions, further wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Large-grained weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: With the end of the precipitation, the natural avalanche activity will gradually decrease. Along the border with France and on wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high. The avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses. In the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 4 (high) the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes.
At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. They are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

New snow and wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. Additionally avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 15 cm of snow fell on Wednesday above approximately 1800 m. In some regions 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall above approximately 2000 m.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow significantly. The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on shady slopes.

The unusual weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Large-grained weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The sometimes strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northwesterly directions, wind slabs will form over a wide area. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain especially above the tree line.

New snow and wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach large size.

Additionally avalanches can also be released in deep layers and reach very large size in isolated cases in particular on shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

Meticulous route selection is required. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow fell on Wednesday above approximately 1500 m. Over a wide area 15 to 30 cm of snow will fall on Thursday above approximately 1800 m. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from northwesterly directions, further wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Large-grained weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The sometimes strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being. Along the border between Valais and Italy and on wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering at intermediate and high altitudes. Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Along the border with France light precipitation.

More recent wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in all aspects and generally at elevated altitudes. This applies in particular on steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanches can be released in the new snow and wind slab layers and reach large size. Individual gliding avalanches can also occur.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular along the border with France in some localities up to 15 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 2200 m. As a consequence of high temperatures and rain up to intermediate altitudes the snowpack could not consolidate during the last few days.
Above approximately 1500 m a lot of snow is lying.
The unusual weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: With the easing of the wind, the natural avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 4 (high) the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind, avalanche prone wind slabs will form on Thursday. Natural avalanches are possible. Especially on very steep slopes the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Thursday above approximately 1800 m.
The weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. The strong wind will transport the new snow.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects represent the main danger. With the end of the precipitation, the natural avalanche activity will decrease.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses. Above the tree line a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes.
At elevated altitudes these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. They are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.

New snow and wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant or triggered naturally. Additionally avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1800 m. Above approximately 1200 m snow will fall on Thursday. The strong wind will transport the new snow and, in some cases, old snow as well.
The wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on shady slopes.

Individual weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on shady slopes. This applies in particular at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be quite prone to triggering. At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail. As a consequence of new snow and wind the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some places be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche-prone wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground. They can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of the precipitation.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be clear at times.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering. Fresh wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. They can be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
As a consequence of the occasionally strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. These are in some cases prone to triggering on northwest to north to east facing aspects.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall in some regions. The wind will be strong in some regions. The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on surface hoar in some places above the tree line. This applies in particular on northwest, north and east facing slopes.

Above the tree line: The snowpack will be prone to triggering. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Below the tree line as well as steep sunny slopes: The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. A sometimes treacherous avalanche situation will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering. Fresh wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can as before be released easily. Mostly they are medium-sized. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

As a consequence of the occasionally strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. These are in some cases prone to triggering in particular on northwest to north to east facing aspects.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall in some regions. The wind will be strong over a wide area.

The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on surface hoar in some places above the tree line. This applies in particular on northwest, north and east facing slopes.

Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m as well as steep sunny slopes: The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. A sometimes treacherous avalanche situation will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.
Over a wide area easily released wind slabs formed. The wind slabs are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. In particular on very steep slopes and in gullies and bowls medium-sized avalanches are possible.
Over a wide area up to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the past few hours above approximately 1500 m.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended. Experience and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar above the tree line. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.

bulletins.highlights: The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. The avalanche danger above the tree line is within the uppermost range of danger level 3 (considerable). In addition the wind slabs should be taken into account.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
In addition the wind slabs in all aspects are prone to triggering in many locations.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes. The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced over a wide area. The snowpack will be moist at low and intermediate altitudes.
Some snow fell on Wednesday over a wide area.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will persist. Above the tree line a treacherous avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially on steep shady slopes adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. As a consequence of the occasionally strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form. These are in some cases prone to triggering. In some cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall. The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

Low and intermediate altitudes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible below approximately 1900 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible. In particular transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are treacherous.

snowpackStructure.comment: In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places new snow is lying on a hard crust.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The fresh wind slabs can be released in isolated cases in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
As a consequence of the precipitation moist loose snow slides are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.
